steps.title: Find what would block counting
steps.content: The researcher claims the ring-counting method often cannot identify the age of olive trees. Since the method requires looking at a stump and counting dark rings, support must show why those rings cannot be counted reliably.

steps.title: Exclude irrelevant facts
steps.content: Choice A concerns the possible age of one tree, not whether rings can be counted. Choice B explains what narrow rings can indicate, not whether they are visible. Choice D gives climate conditions where olive trees thrive. The answer is C.
